title: Production Hardening: Cloudflare Tunnel Ingress, RBAC, Resource Limits, and Security for PM Server
subtasks: Create an accesstunnel or clustertunnel Custom Resource in the sigma-1-dev namespace to expose the PM server via Cloudflare Tunnel. Map /api/* to the PM server service. TLS terminates at Cloudflare edge. Do NOT deploy NGINX or any other ingress controller.

Configure a Cloudflare Access application on the tunnel to restrict access to authorized users (SSO/MFA at the edge). If the cluster supports declarative Access CRDs, use them; otherwise, document dashboard steps.
Harden the sigma-1-pm-server ServiceAccount RBAC: ensure Role/RoleBinding grants only get/list/watch on configmaps and secrets in sigma-1-dev. Remove any cluster-wide permissions.
Set CPU/memory requests and limits on the PM server deployment: requests cpu=256m, memory=512Mi; limits cpu=1000m, memory=1Gi.
Configure livenessProbe (HTTP GET /health, 10s interval) and readinessProbe (HTTP GET /ready, 5s interval) on the PM server deployment.

Create NetworkPolicy resources: default-deny egress for all pods in sigma-1-dev; allow PM server to reach bridge services (discord-bridge-http, linear-bridge), GitHub API, Hermes API, Linear API, and DNS. Block all other egress.
Enable Kubernetes audit logging for create/update/delete on secrets, configmaps, and deployments in sigma-1-dev. Document log location and query method.
Set external-secrets operator refreshInterval to 24h for all ExternalSecret resources in sigma-1-secrets. Add annotations for rotation alerting.
Document Helm values for scaling PM server to 2+ replicas and PodDisruptionBudget for production. For dev, single replica is acceptable.
If D5 resolves to defer frontend, remove frontend-related ingress routes, ServiceAccount, resource limits, and NetworkPolicies from manifests and documentation.
